commit.message: Added Memori heading.

Add Memori MCP and OpenClaw documentation (#351)

* Add Memori MCP and OpenClaw documentation

This PR introduces two major documentation additions for Memori Cloud:

1. Memori MCP docs
2. OpenClaw docs

It also cleans up older docs that referenced manual augmentation waiting behavior.

## Changes

- Added Memori MCP documentation:
  - overview of MCP usage with Memori
  - client setup instructions across Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Warp, and Antigravity
  - agent skills (`SKILL.md`) guidance for reliable recall + augmentation behavior

- Added OpenClaw documentation:
  - overview of OpenClaw memory limitations and Memori plugin architecture
  - quickstart for plugin install, config, verification, and expected logs

- Minor formatting updates

* Update docs as per feedback

---------

Co-authored-by: Ryan Kruse <rpkruse1@gmail.com>

commit.message:
OpenClaw Plugin Beta (#334)

* Add OpenClaw Memori integration and workflows

Introduce a new OpenClaw plugin that integrates Memori long-term memory (integrations/openclaw). Adds source code, README, lint/Prettier configs, plugin manifest and TypeScript project files. Add CI and publish GitHub Actions (ts-openclaw-ci.yml and publish-openclaw-npm.yml) which generate src/version.ts, run lint/build, and publish npm package. Update .gitignore and MANIFEST.in to include the integration and adjust packaging; small project metadata changes applied.

* Make OpenClaw Memori plugin stateless

Refactor the OpenClaw Memori integration to a stateless architecture: remove local session manager and caching, simplify constants and types, and initialize the Memori client directly with apiKey per request. Update handler signatures to accept a MemoriPluginConfig and a MemoriLogger, and wire those through the plugin registration. Enforce required apiKey and entityId in openclaw.plugin.json, add UI hints and package keywords, and overhaul README content and docs. Also improve sanitizer logic and consolidate utility exports (memori client moved to accept apiKey).

* OpenClaw: improve message parsing and context handling

Refactor OpenClaw integration to make augmentation and context extraction more robust.

- Augmentation: rewrite handler to process recent messages, clean and normalize content, strip routing tags, handle special assistant tokens (NO_REPLY/SILENT_REPLY) with a synthetic reply, and skip augmentation for missing/invalid messages.
- Sanitizer: tighten message block detection, add extractRawUserMessage to pull content after OpenClaw metadata fences, remove injected memori_context tags, and simplify cleaning logic.
- Context utils: remove fallback defaults and require explicit entity/session/provider resolution; throw clear errors when required context is missing.
- Constants: remove unused DEFAULTS export.
- Minor fixes: correct stray brace in log message and compress plugin JSON required array formatting.

Overall this improves reliability of memory capture by ensuring messages and context are properly parsed and validated before sending to the client.

* openclaw: refactor augmentation, constants, and utils

Remove test-specific ESLint exceptions and adjust ignores; add package.json files entry to include dist and plugin manifest. Pull augmentation limits into constants (AUGMENTATION_CONFIG) and update handler to use configurable MAX_CONTEXT_MESSAGES, use findLast for message selection, and simplify assistant tag stripping with a regex. Introduce a TIMESTAMP_PREFIX_REGEX in sanitizer and reuse it for timestamp stripping. Tighten context extraction: return configured entityId directly and improve error checks/messages for missing session/provider.

* Cleanup comments and fix logging finalizers

Remove verbose comments/docblocks and tidy up code across the OpenClaw Memori integration. Add try/finally blocks in augmentation and recall handlers to ensure logger.endSection is always called, normalize some log messages, simplify constants/types/sanitizer by removing inline comments, and remove leftover commented hook lines in index.ts. These changes improve logging reliability and reduce comment noise in the codebase.

* Update package-lock.json

* Aug update to make it better, minor format changes

* Removed auto release for publish for memori-ts and oc

* force package-lock update

commit.message:
Introduce changes to make plugin integration easier in the future (#330)

* Introduce IntegrationRequest and rename capture

Add a unified IntegrationRequest/IntegrationMetadata type and refactor integration APIs to use it. BaseIntegration now exposes executeAugmentation (replacing executeCapture) and builds synthetic LLM requests/responses from IntegrationRequest, passing metadata into CallContext. AugmentationEngine now prefers ctx.metadata.integrationSdkVersion for sdk.version and ctx.metadata.platform for platform. OpenClawIntegration and the public exports were updated to use the new types and method name (capture -> augmentation). Tests updated to assert metadata propagation and adjusted to the new API.

* Add TS doc to integration types

commit.message:
Add TypeScript memori-ts SDK  (#311)

* Add TypeScript memori-ts SDK and CI workflows

Introduce a new memori-ts TypeScript SDK package (src, tests, README, example, package.json, .npmignore, eslint config) implementing core config, network client, session management, errors, recall/persistence/augmentation engines, utils, and Axon integration. Add CI and publish workflows (.github/workflows/ts-ci.yml and publish-npm.yml), update .gitignore, and extend pre-commit hooks to run formatting, linting, typecheck, and tests for the memori-ts package. This enables automated checks, tests, and npm publishing for the new SDK.

* Add memori-ts package-lock and update .gitignore

Un-ignore package-lock.json in .gitignore and add memori-ts/package-lock.json to source control. This ensures the memori-ts package lock is tracked for reproducible installs.

Add TypeScript and Prettier configuration files for the memori-ts package and update .gitignore to ensure they are tracked.

- Add memori-ts/tsconfig.json with strict compiler options, declarations, and include/exclude entries.
- Add memori-ts/tsconfig.build.json extending tsconfig.json with build-specific options (rootDir, outDir, declaration, sourceMap).
- Add memori-ts/.prettierrc.json with project formatting rules.
- Update top-level .gitignore to whitelist tsconfig.json, tsconfig.build.json, and .prettierrc.json so these config files are not ignored.

* Use explicit Memori return type for register

Change the TypeScript signature of Memori.register from returning 'this' to returning 'Memori'. This makes the method's return type explicit (in memori-ts/src/memori.ts), improving type clarity and IDE/type-checking when using or chaining the register call.

* Improve README quickstart and bump version

Revamp the TypeScript SDK README with a clearer quickstart: add dotenv/OpenAI import, environment validation, a two-step teach-and-recall example, and explicit session handling. Add notes about supported LLMs, key features, advanced augmentation, quota management, and clarify attribution wording. Also update example snippets and minor copy edits. Bump package version from 0.1.0 to 0.1.1b1.

* Publish beta tag and bump package version

Prepare a beta release: add the --tag beta flag to the GitHub Actions npm publish step to publish pre-release packages. Bump the package version in package.json to 0.1.1-beta and update the @memorilabs/axon dependency to the matching beta version. Also apply minor README formatting and newline fixes.

* Apply PR change requets

Add CI steps to generate src/version.ts from package.json and bump GitHub Action usages (checkout@v5, setup-node@v6). Introduce src/version.ts (dev default) and replace hardcoded SDK version with SDK_VERSION across engines. Move extractLastUserMessage into utils and reuse it from augmentation, persistence and recall engines. Rename HostedRecallResponse to CloudRecallResponse and update related utility and engine types. Minor cleanup: adjust example path and attribution, update package.json example script, export UnsupportedLLMProviderError from core/errors, remove duplicate re-export in index, and bump TS target/lib to ES2023.

Add a matrix strategy to the TypeScript CI workflow to run tests across Node versions [18, 20, 22, 24]. Parameterize the setup-node step to use the matrix value (and update the step name accordingly) instead of the previously hardcoded node 24.

* Add LLM metadata from CallContext and bump axon

Pass CallContext into augmentation metadata generation and populate llm fields (provider and sdk.version) from ctx.metadata. Update handleAugmentation to accept ctx and forward it to buildMeta. Adjust augmentation tests to provide a mock context. Extend utils tests: add extractLastUserMessage tests, cover extractFacts fallback to similarity and ignoring invalid entries, and add additional extractHistory cases. Bump @memorilabs/axon dependency to ^0.1.2-beta in package.json and package-lock.json.

* Add req.model to SDK metadata

Include the request model in the SDK metadata by adding `version: req.model || null` to the `sdk` object. This ensures the augmentation engine records which model was used for the request (falls back to null when absent).

commit.message:
Fix: Lazy-import psycopg (#306)

* Lazy-import psycopg and add optional deps

Remove module-level psycopg import and perform runtime imports with clear ImportError messages in Memori and ClusterManager to avoid requiring psycopg for users who don't use CockroachDB/Postgres. Move psycopg from required dependencies to project optional-dependencies (postgres and cockroachdb extras) in pyproject.toml. Update tests to patch psycopg.connect at the top-level import path accordingly.

* Use MissingPsycopgError for CockroachDB

Add a MissingPsycopgError exception and raise it when psycopg is missing instead of a raw ImportError. Updated imports in memori.__init__ and the CockroachDB cluster manager to use the new exception. Also removed the postgres/cockroachdb optional-dependencies entries from pyproject.toml. This makes the missing dependency error clearer and centralizes the install guidance for psycopg (suggesting memori[postgres] or memori[cockroachdb]).

* Add optional cockroachdb dependency

Add a project.optional-dependencies extras group named 'cockroachdb' that requires psycopg[binary]>=3.1.0. This provides an opt-in dependency for CockroachDB/Postgres driver support without making it a hard dependency for all users.

commit.message:
Hosted metrics (#299)

* feat: add performance benchmarks for hosted recall API

- Introduced a new benchmark suite for testing the performance and latency of the hosted recall path using the Memori API.
- Implemented multiple tests to measure end-to-end latency, pre-LLM overhead, and network history retrieval.
- Added configuration and message seeding functions to facilitate benchmarking.
- Ensured that benchmarks can be run conditionally based on the presence of the MEMORI_API_KEY environment variable.

- Replaced the direct lambda method for fetching hosted conversation messages with a new subclass, _InvokeWithStubbedHistory, to encapsulate history management.
- Enhanced the structure of the invoke function to better support testing with stubbed history, improving clarity and maintainability of the benchmark code.

commit.message:
fix: automatic memory injection for Google GenAI provider (#215) (#221)

This commit fixes two critical issues that prevented memory injection
from working with the Google GenAI SDK:

1. _extract_user_query now handles Google GenAI's 'contents' parameter:
   - Supports string contents (simple query)
   - Supports list of strings
   - Supports structured contents format with parts/role

2. inject_recalled_facts now properly injects context for Google GenAI:
   - If config.system_instruction is available, appends context there
   - Otherwise, prepends context to the first user message's content
   - If first message is not a user role, inserts a new context message
   - Properly normalizes contents format (string/list/structured)
   - Ensures kwargs['contents'] is properly updated

Also fixed a minor bug where kwargs['messages'] wasn't being assigned back
in the else branch for non-Anthropic/Bedrock providers.

Includes comprehensive tests for all Google GenAI scenarios:
- String contents
- List of strings
- Structured contents with parts/role
- system_instruction config
- Edge case: first message not user role

readme_html:
Getting Started

Installation

npm install @memorilabs/memori

pip install memori

Quickstart

Sign up at app.memorilabs.ai, get a Memori API key, and start building. Full docs: memorilabs.ai/docs/memori-cloud/.

Set MEMORI_API_KEY and your LLM API key (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY), then:

import { OpenAI } from 'openai';
import { Memori } from '@memorilabs/memori';

// Requires MEMORI_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment
const client = new OpenAI();
const mem = new Memori().llm
  .register(client)
  .attribution('user_123', 'support_agent');

async function main() {
  await client.chat.completions.create({
    model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
    messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'My favorite color is blue.' }],
  });
  // Conversations are persisted and recalled automatically in the background.

  const response = await client.chat.completions.create({
    model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
    messages: [{ role: 'user', content: "What's my favorite color?" }],
  });
  // Memori recalls that your favorite color is blue.
}

from memori import Memori
from openai import OpenAI

# Requires MEMORI_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment
client = OpenAI()
mem = Memori().llm.register(client)

mem.attribution(entity_id="user_123", process_id="support_agent")

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "My favorite color is blue."}]
)
# Conversations are persisted and recalled automatically.

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What's my favorite color?"}]
)
# Memori recalls that your favorite color is blue.

Explore the Memories

Use the Dashboard — Memories, Analytics, Playground, and API Keys.

[!TIP] Want to use your own database? Check out docs for Memori BYODB here: https://memorilabs.ai/docs/memori-byodb/.

Attribution

To get the most out of Memori, you want to attribute your LLM interactions to an entity (think person, place or thing; like a user) and a process (think your agent, LLM interaction or program).

If you do not provide any attribution, Memori cannot make memories for you.

mem.attribution("12345", "my-ai-bot");

mem.attribution(entity_id="12345", process_id="my-ai-bot")

Session Management

Memori uses sessions to group your LLM interactions together. For example, if you have an agent that executes multiple steps you want those to be recorded in a single session.

By default, Memori handles setting the session for you but you can start a new session or override the session by executing the following:

mem.resetSession();
# or
mem.setSession(sessionId);

mem.new_session()
# or
mem.set_session(session_id)

Supported LLMs

  • Anthropic
  • Bedrock
  • DeepSeek
  • Gemini
  • Grok (xAI)
  • OpenAI (Chat Completions & Responses API)

(unstreamed, streamed, synchronous and asynchronous)

Supported Frameworks

  • Agno
  • LangChain
  • Pydantic AI

Supported Platforms

  • DeepSeek
  • Nebius AI Studio

Examples

For more examples and demos, check out the Memori Cookbook.

Memori Advanced Augmentation

Memories are tracked at several different levels:

  • entity: think person, place, or thing; like a user
  • process: think your agent, LLM interaction or program
  • session: the current interactions between the entity, process and the LLM

Memori's Advanced Augmentation enhances memories at each of these levels with:

  • attributes
  • events
  • facts
  • people
  • preferences
  • relationships
  • rules
  • skills

Memori knows who your user is, what tasks your agent handles and creates unparalleled context between the two. Augmentation occurs in the background incurring no latency.

By default, Memori Advanced Augmentation is available without an account but rate limited. When you need increased limits, sign up for Memori Advanced Augmentation or use the Memori CLI:

# Install the CLI via pip to manage your account
python -m memori sign-up <email_address>

Memori Advanced Augmentation is always free for developers!

Once you've obtained an API key, set the following environment variable (used by both Python and TypeScript SDKs):

export MEMORI_API_KEY=[api_key]

Managing Your Quota

At any time, you can check your quota using the Memori CLI (works for both SDKs):

python -m memori quota

Or by checking your account at https://app.memorilabs.ai/. If you have reached your IP address quota, sign up and get an API key for increased limits.

If your API key exceeds its quota limits we will email you and let you know.

Command Line Interface (CLI)

The Memori CLI is the unified tool for managing your account, keys, and quotas across all SDKs. To use it, execute the following from the command line:
